 {"id":"bd-mk1i5","title":"Tier 1: Safety & Reliability Integrations","description":"EPIC for Tier 1 integrations focused on immediate safety and reliability improvements.

## Why Tier 1 is Highest Priority

These three integrations address the most critical pain points in multi-agent workflows:

1. **DCG**: Prevents catastrophic mistakes. A single 'rm -rf /' or 'git push --force origin main' from any agent can destroy hours of work or worse. DCG blocks these at the command level before they execute.

2. **CAAM**: Rate limits are the #1 operational pain point. When Claude hits rate limits, you wait hours. CAAM has OAuth tokens for multiple accounts and can switch in <100ms, letting work continue immediately.

3. **caut**: Cost visibility prevents bill shock. 16+ providers tracked. Know when you're approaching limits before you hit them.

## Integration Approach

All three are Rust CLIs that work via subprocess invocation:
- DCG: Hook into PreToolUse (already exists in claude-code ecosystem)
- CAAM: Add --robot-switch-account command to NTM robot mode
- caut: Add --robot-quota-status and dashboard widget

## Success Metrics

- [ ] Zero destructive commands execute across managed agents (DCG)
- [ ] Rate limit recovery time < 1 minute (CAAM)
- [ ] Quota visibility in dashboard with alerts at 80% threshold (caut)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:43:27.049682004Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T18:00:12.558020123Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T18:00:12.557947457Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mk1i5","depends_on_id":"bd-3fg81","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-n5hu","title":"Task: Format and inject recovery prompt","description":"## Purpose
Synthesize all recovery context into a formatted prompt for agent injection.

## Recovery Prompt Template
# Session Recovery Context

## Your Previous Work
- You were working on: [bead title from BV]
- Last checkpoint: [timestamp] — [description]
- Files you were editing: [from Agent Mail reservations]

## Recent Messages
[Last 3 Agent Mail messages from relevant threads]

## Key Decisions Made
[From CM procedural memory for this project]

## Current Task Status
- [x] Completed: [completed beads]
- [ ] In progress: [in_progress beads]
- [ ] Blocked by: [blocking issues]

Reread AGENTS.md and continue from where you left off.

## Scope
- Format RecoveryContext into markdown prompt
- Apply token limit (config: max_recovery_tokens = 2000)
- Prioritize most relevant information if truncating
- Send to agent pane on spawn

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery prompt readable and actionable
- Respects token limit configuration
- Graceful when partial context available
- Sent before user's first prompt

## Technical Notes
Use template/text for formatting
Send via existing send mechanism","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:35:17.291725081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:43:05.409285011Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:43:05.409240778Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: FormatRecoveryPrompt function synthesizes context into markdown, token limits enforced via truncateRecoveryContext, graceful degradation tested, injection happens on spawn. All 27 recovery tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n5hu","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-n770","title":"Add YAML swarm config file - define entire swarm (agents, prompts, assignments) in one declarative file","description":"## Overview
Define entire swarm configuration in one declarative YAML file.

## Config Format (swarm.yaml)
```yaml
name: myproject-swarm
session: myproject

agents:
  claude: 10
  codex: 10  
  gemini: 4
  
options:
  no-user: true
  auto-assign: true
  
prompts:
  init: |
    Read AGENTS.md and register with Agent Mail.
    Check your inbox and respond to any messages.
    Use bv --robot-triage to find work.
  review: |
    Review code written by fellow agents...
  post-compaction: |
    Reread AGENTS.md. Use ultrathink.

assignment:
  source: bv
  limit: 24
  template: impl
  round-robin: false
  
monitor:
  stuck_threshold_seconds: 300
  auto_restart_stuck: true
  completion_patterns:
    - 'bead complete'
    - 'task finished'
```

## Commands
```bash
ntm swarm --config=swarm.yaml           # Start full swarm
ntm spawn --config=swarm.yaml           # Just spawn from config
ntm assign --config=swarm.yaml          # Use assignment settings
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses YAML config format
- [ ] Validates required fields
- [ ] Supports all spawn options
- [ ] Supports prompt templates
- [ ] Supports assignment settings
- [ ] Supports monitor settings
- [ ] Clear error messages for invalid config

## Tests Required
- Unit test: YAML parsing and validation
- Unit test: Config defaults
- E2E test: Full swarm from config file","notes":"Closed as dropped: avoid new YAML format; reuse existing pipeline/recipe configs if declarative setup is needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:40:47.613482061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:16:34.562867377Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T19:09:17.646542265Z","close_reason":"Dropped per canonical assign flow; redundant/overlapping entry points","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n770","depends_on_id":"bd-2rqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-n9bcu","title":"Feature: Prompt Engine - Mode-specific preambles and injection","description":"# Feature: Prompt Engine - Mode-specific preambles with schema enforcement

Each mode agent receives a structured prompt with three mandatory components:
1. Base instructions (tone, structure, constraints)
2. Mode preamble (the reasoning 'lens')
3. **Output schema contract** (mandatory structured output)

## Prompt Structure

### 1. Base Instructions
```
You are participating in a reasoning ensemble analyzing: {problem_statement}

Context Pack:
{context_pack}

Your role: Apply your assigned reasoning mode rigorously.

Constraints:
- Stay within your reasoning mode's strengths
- Be explicit about confidence levels (numeric or high|medium|low)
- Reference specific evidence (file:line)
- Flag your mode's known failure modes
- DO NOT exceed {token_cap} tokens
```

### 2. Mode Preamble (The Lens)
```
## Your Reasoning Mode: {mode_name}

{mode_description}

What you excel at:
{best_for}

How you differ from other modes:
{differentiator}

Common failure modes to avoid:
{failure_modes}
```

### 3. Output Schema Contract (MANDATORY)
```
## REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT

You MUST structure your response as valid YAML with these exact fields:

mode_id: {mode_id}           # optional; system will inject if missing
thesis: |
  Your one-sentence main insight
confidence: 0.0-1.0           # numeric or high|medium|low

top_findings:
  - finding: Description of finding
    impact: critical|high|medium|low
    confidence: 0.0-1.0        # numeric or high|medium|low
    evidence_pointer: path/to/file.go:142
    reasoning: How you reached this

risks:
  - risk: Description of risk
    impact: critical|high|medium|low
    likelihood: 0.0-1.0        # numeric or high|medium|low
    mitigation: Suggested mitigation
    affected_areas: [string]

recommendations:
  - recommendation: What to do
    priority: critical|high|medium|low
    effort: low|medium|high
    rationale: Why this helps
    related_findings: [int]    # indices into top_findings (0-based)

questions_for_user:
  - question: What needs clarification?
    context: Why this matters
    blocking: false
    suggested_answers: [string]

failure_modes_to_watch:
  - mode: Potential failure of my analysis
    description: What could go wrong
    indicators: [string]
    prevention: How to guard against it

NON-COMPLIANT OUTPUTS WILL BE REJECTED.
```

## Schema Validation

PromptInjector validates all outputs:
- Parse YAML structure
- Check required fields present
- Validate enum values (impact/priority/etc.)
- Accept numeric or qualitative likelihood/confidence values
- Ensure evidence_pointers are valid file references
- Validate list fields when present (affected_areas, related_findings, suggested_answers, indicators)
- Inject mode_id if missing (warn)
- Flag non-compliant outputs for human review

## Testing Coverage
- Unit: bd-xkrnc (preamble rendering + schema contract fields)
- Unit: bd-qeoex (schema validator exhaustive cases)
- E2E: bd-18ds7 (full pipeline includes preamble injection + schema validation)

## Files
- internal/ensemble/preamble.go - Template structure
- internal/ensemble/preambles/ - Mode-specific content
- internal/ensemble/schema_validator.go - Output validation
- internal/swarm/prompt_injector.go - Injection logic

## Logging Requirements
- Log prompt assembly start with mode_id, mode_code, pane, agent_type, token_cap
- Log schema contract version/hash and missing required fields
- Log validation summary (valid/invalid counts, first error per mode)
- Log injection outcome per pane (success/failure + error)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prompt assembly deterministic for same inputs (stable ordering)
- [ ] Output schema contract matches OutputSchema types (no drift)
- [ ] Schema validation rejects non-compliant outputs and reports errors
- [ ] Logging captures per-mode injection and validation status","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:25:44.569622243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T00:44:13.143101802Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T00:44:13.142546325Z","close_reason":"Feature complete - Prompt Engine with mode-specific preambles and schema enforcement:

## Implementation Summary
All components verified complete:
1. **preamble.go** - PreambleEngine with base instructions template and schema contract
2. **injector.go** - EnsembleInjector wrapping BasicInjector for mode-aware injection
3. **schema_validator.go** - Full validation of ModeOutput with ParseYAML/Normalize/Validate
4. **types.go** - OutputSchema types (ModeOutput, Finding, Risk, Recommendation, Question, FailureModeWarning)

## Acceptance Criteria Verified
- [x] Prompt assembly deterministic (Go templates with stable ordering)
- [x] Schema contract matches OutputSchema types (all fields aligned)
- [x] Schema validation rejects non-compliant outputs with detailed errors
- [x] Logging captures mode_id, mode_code, pane, agent_type, success/failure

## Key Features
- Three-section preamble: base instructions + mode section + schema contract
- Context pack rendering with project brief and user context
- Mode metadata auto-generation from ReasoningMode fields
- SchemaVersion tracking (1.0.0)
- Support for numeric or qualitative confidence/likelihood values","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-1hlhd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-2pl9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-4io74","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-qeoex","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-wx2qv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-n9bcu","depends_on_id":"bd-xkrnc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
